text: Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.
author: Carl Sagan

text: If you wish to make an apple pie from scratch, you must first invent the universe.
author: Carl Sagan

text: You are a way for the cosmos to know itself.
author: Carl Sagan

text: The universe is under no obligation to make sense to you.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: Not only is the universe stranger than we imagine, it is stranger than we can imagine.
author: J.B.S. Haldane

text: The good thing about science is that it's true whether or not you believe in it.
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson

text: What we know is a drop. What we don't know is an ocean.
author: Isaac Newton

text: The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
author: Richard Feynman

text: If I could explain it to the average person, I wouldn't have been worth the Nobel Prize.
author: Richard Feynman

text: Imagination will take you everywhere.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Make things as simple as possible, but not simpler.
author: Albert Einstein

text: God does not play dice with the universe — but the universe may be playing a game far stranger than dice.
author: Einstein (extended paraphrase)

text: I think it is safe to say that no one understands quantum mechanics.
author: Richard Feynman

text: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff.
author: Carl Sagan

text: Philosophy is not a distraction from physics; physics is a refinement of philosophy.
author: Raman

text: Some infinities are bigger than other infinities.
author: Cantor (popular paraphrase)

text: Nature is pleased with simplicity. And nature is no dummy.
author: Isaac Newton

text: In questions of science, the authority of a thousand is not worth the humble reasoning of a single individual.
author: Galileo Galilei

text: Time is what prevents everything from happening at once.
author: John Wheeler

text: It from bit.
author: John Wheeler

text: The boundary of a boundary is zero — and so is the boundary of your limitations when you learn.
author: Raman

text: Mathematics is the music of reason.
author: James Joseph Sylvester

text: What is proved by impossibility proofs is lack of imagination.
author: John von Neumann

text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
author: Alan Kay

text: We only understand the world because it is structured in a mathematical way.
author: Roger Penrose

text: Black holes aren't the end. They're the beginning of understanding.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: The universe doesn't allow perfection.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: No amount of evidence will ever persuade an idiot.
author: Mark Twain

text: There is geometry in the humming of the strings.
author: Pythagoras

text: All models are wrong, but some are useful.
author: George Box

text: You never change things by fighting the existing reality. Build a new model that makes the old one obsolete.
author: Buckminster Fuller

text: He who thinks great thoughts, often makes great errors.
author: Martin Heidegger

text: What we call chaos is just patterns we haven't recognized.
author: Chuck Palahniuk

text: Order and chaos are not enemies; they are dance partners.
author: Raman

text: If you are the smartest person in the room, you are in the wrong room.
author: Confucius (attributed)

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: He who has a why can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: When you gaze long into an abyss, the abyss also gazes into you.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The mind is everything. What you think, you become.
author: Buddha

text: Three things cannot be long hidden: the sun, the moon, and the truth.
author: Buddha

text: To understand the immeasurable, the mind must be extraordinarily quiet.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: A man is great not because he hasn’t failed, but because failure hasn’t stopped him.
author: Confucius

text: Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: The best revenge is not to be like your enemy.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: We suffer more in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: To be calm is the highest achievement of the self.
author: Zen proverb

text: The flame that burns twice as bright burns half as long.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Do not mistake tranquility for stagnation.
author: Raman

text: If you don't build your own dreams, someone else will hire you to build theirs.
author: Tony Gaskins

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Entropy is the price of structure.
author: Raman

text: In the end, everything is a probability distribution.
author: Raman

text: Wheresoever you go, go with all your heart.
author: Confucius

text: A ship is safe in harbor, but that's not what ships are built for.
author: John A. Shedd

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence is not an act but a habit.
author: Aristotle (paraphrased)

text: Do not pray for an easy life. Pray for the strength to endure a difficult one.
author: Bruce Lee

text: Be like water.
author: Bruce Lee

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: Some people are born into freedom; others carve it out of the chaos.
author: Raman

text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

text: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will rule your life.
author: Carl Jung

text: What is essential is invisible to the eye.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

text: If you're going through hell, keep going.
author: Winston Churchill

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Reality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Be humble, for you are made of earth. Be noble, for you are made of stars.
author: Serbian proverb

text: Stars don't beg the world for attention. They shine.
author: Unknown

text: He who has no patience will never ascend the mountain.
author: Raman

text: Mastery is not a final state; it is the act of continuous becoming.
author: Raman

text: Everything is temporary. But temporary things can change the universe.
author: Raman

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Your assumptions are your windows on the world. Scrub them occasionally.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Violence is the last refuge of the incompetent.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Information wants to be free.
author: Stewart Brand

text: Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic.
author: Arthur C. Clarke

text: The real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new landscapes, but in having new eyes.
author: Marcel Proust

text: The greatest enemy of knowledge is not ignorance, it is the illusion of knowledge.
author: Stephen Hawking

text: To think deeply in our world today is to be dangerous.
author: Raman

text: Survival is not enough. Meaning must follow.
author: Raman

text: If the universe was created only once, it can be done again.
author: Raman

text: There are no shortcuts to any place worth going.
author: Beverly Sills

text: An expert is a person who has made all the mistakes that can be made in a very narrow field.
author: Niels Bohr

text: Opposites are complementary — entropy and order define each other.
author: Raman

text: The most beautiful thing we can experience is the mysterious.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Science is the poetry of reality.
author: Richard Dawkins

text: You are not obligated to complete the work, but neither are you free to abandon it.
author: Rabbi Tarfon

text: The world breaks everyone, and afterward, many are strong at the broken places.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: There is nothing noble in being superior to your fellow man; true nobility is being superior to your former self.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: To understand the world, you must first understand yourself.
author: Raman

text: Purpose is stronger than fear.
author: Raman
